preamble: The shift from 'ts' to 'ch' (tʃ) is a classic example of palatalization, where an alveolar affricate moves to a post-alveolar position, often triggered by following high vowels or palatal glides.

examples.note: Development of Slavic affricates before high vowels.
Historical development in various Romance dialects before high vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
